From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Basketball team in Limburg, Belgium

**Limburg United**, for sponsorship reasons named **Hubo Limburg United**, is a professional [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") club based in [Hasselt](/wiki/Hasselt "Hasselt"), [Limburg](/wiki/Limburg_(Belgium) "Limburg (Belgium)"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium").

Founded in 2014, the club plays in the [BNXT League](/wiki/BNXT_League "BNXT League"), the first tier of basketball in Belgium. The team plays its home games in Sporthal Alverberg. Limburg has won one trophy, the [Belgian Cup](/wiki/Belgian_Basketball_Cup "Belgian Basketball Cup") in [2022](/w/index.php?title=2021%E2%80%9322_Belgian_Basketball_Cup&action=edit&redlink=1 "2021–22 Belgian Basketball Cup (page does not exist)").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Limburg_United&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Around the beginning of 2014 Limburg United was founded in the city of Hasselt. The name expressing the founders' motivation for there to be a top tier basketball team from [Limburg](/wiki/Limburg_(Belgium) "Limburg (Belgium)") province in the aftermath of [Bree B.B.C.](/wiki/Bree_B.B.C. "Bree B.B.C.") relegating from [BLB Division I](/wiki/BLB_Division_I "BLB Division I") in 2008 and eventually disbanding. Limburg United asked for a B-licence for the [2014–15 Basketball League Belgium](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Basketball_League_Belgium "2014–15 Basketball League Belgium"), and eventually got it. Former [Antwerp Giants](/wiki/Antwerp_Giants "Antwerp Giants") assistant [Brian Lynch](/wiki/Brian_Lynch_(basketball) "Brian Lynch (basketball)") signed a 5-year contract to become United's head coach.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The long-term goal of the club was expressed to "become a club in the subtop of Belgium in 3 or 5 years."[[3]](#cite_note-3) In the 2016–17 season, Limburg reached the Final of the [Belgian Basketball Cup](/wiki/Belgian_Basketball_Cup "Belgian Basketball Cup") but lost to [Telenet Oostende](/wiki/Telenet_Oostende "Telenet Oostende").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Since the [2021–22 season](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_BNXT_League "2021–22 BNXT League"), United plays in the [BNXT League](/wiki/BNXT_League "BNXT League"), in which the national leagues of Belgium and the Netherlands have been merged.[[5]](#cite_note-5) On 14 March 2022, Limburg United won its first-ever trophy when it captured the [Belgian Cup](/wiki/Belgian_Basketball_Cup "Belgian Basketball Cup") after defeating Oostende in the final.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Limburg_United&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Honours")]

[Belgian Basketball Cup](/wiki/Belgian_Basketball_Cup "Belgian Basketball Cup")

* **Champions (2):** [2021–22](/w/index.php?title=2021%E2%80%9322_Belgian_Basketball_Cup&action=edit&redlink=1 "2021–22 Belgian Basketball Cup (page does not exist)"), [2023–24](/wiki/2023%E2%80%9324_Belgian_Basketball_Cup "2023–24 Belgian Basketball Cup")
* Runner-up (1): [2016–17](/wiki/2016-17_Belgian_Basketball_Cup "2016-17 Belgian Basketball Cup")

## Sponsorship names

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Limburg_United&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Sponsorship names")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/6/6e/Limburg_United_logo.png/250px-Limburg_United_logo.png)](/wiki/File:Limburg_United_logo.png)

The original Limburg United logo, as introduced in 2014

Due to sponsorship reasons, the club has been known as:

* **[Hubo](/wiki/Hubo_Belgium "Hubo Belgium") Limburg United** (2014–present)
